message:
🌱 Add rook: On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume. mission (#1772)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,80 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "rook-1507-on-nodelost-the-new-pod-can-t-mount-the-same-volume",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "rook: On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.",
+    "description": "On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.. This issue affects 43+ users.",
+    "type": "troubleshoot",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Identify rook troubleshoot symptoms",
+        "description": "Check for the issue in your rook deployment:\n```bash\nkubectl get pods -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=100 | grep -i error\n```\nLook for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review rook configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant rook configuration:\n```bash\nkubectl get all -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook\nkubectl get configmap -n rook -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=rook\n```\nIs this a bug report or feature request?\n* Bug Report\n\n**Bug Report**\n\nWhat happened:\nA PV was attached to a POD. The node running the POD lost the power. Currently the status for the node is NotReady."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same volume.",
+        "description": "The status for the old pod is `terminating`. Does that pod never go away? Rook doesn't risk attaching to the new pod until the old pod is gone. \n\n what if Rook goes ahead with the mount on [this line](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/pkg/daemon/agent/flexvolume/controller.go#L120) if the\n```yaml\nWarning  FailedMount  16m (x82 over 2h)  kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume \"pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994\" : mount command failed, status: Failure, reason: Rook: Mount volume failed: failed to attach volume pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994 for pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7. Volume is already attached by pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-zlhld. Status Running\n  Warning  FailedMount  2m (x73 over 2h)   kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  Unable to mount volumes for pod \"perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7_perfsonar(2394445f-1\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Confirm On NodeLost, the new pod can't mount the same… is resolved",
+        "description": "Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:\n```bash\nkubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=rook -n rook --tail=50 --since=5m\nkubectl get events -n rook --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm that the issue symptoms are gone."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "The status for the old pod is `terminating`. Does that pod never go away? Rook doesn't risk attaching to the new pod until the old pod is gone. \n\n what if Rook goes ahead with the mount on [this line](https://github.com/rook/rook/blob/master/pkg/daemon/agent/flexvolume/controller.go#L120) if the status is `terminating` and the reason is `NodeLost`?",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Warning  FailedMount  16m (x82 over 2h)  kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  MountVolume.SetUp failed for volume \"pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994\" : mount command failed, status: Failure, reason: Rook: Mount volume failed: failed to attach volume pvc-31b7555e-122d-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994 for pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7. Volume is already attached by pod perfsonar/perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-zlhld. Status Running\n  Warning  FailedMount  2m (x73 over 2h)   kubelet, fiona-dtn-1.ucsc.edu  Unable to mount volumes for pod \"perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7_perfsonar(2394445f-138c-11e8-9422-0cc47a6be994)\": timeout expired waiting for volumes to attach/mount for pod \"perfsonar\"/\"perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-xhzt7\". list of unattached/unmounted volumes=[pgsql-persistent-storag",
+        "Dmitrys-MBP-2:~ dimm$ kubectl describe pod perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-zlhld -n perfsonar\nName:                      perfsonar-toolkit-bdc95f48f-zlhld\nNamespace:                 perfsonar\nNode:                      k8s-chase-ci-06.calit2.optiputer.net/192.0.2.1\nStart Time:                Thu, 15 Feb 2018 00:49:55 -0800\nLabels:                    k8s-app=perfsonar-toolkit\n                           pod-template-hash=687519049\nAnnotations:               kubernetes.io/limit-ranger=LimitRanger plugin set: memory request for container perfsonar-toolkit; memory limit for container perfsonar-toolkit; memory request for init container volume-mount-chown; m...\nStatus:                    Terminating (expires Fri, 16 Feb 2018 18:42:34 -0800)\nTermination Grace Period:  30s\nReason:                  ",
+        "--node-monitor-grace-period=20s (the link suggests 16s)\n--node-monitor-period=2s\n--pod-eviction-timeout=30s\n--node-status-update-frequency=4s"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "rook",
+      "graduated",

+    ],

+    },

+  },
